sentence_reviews.text: A convolutional neural network applies shared local filters over grids or signals, building hierarchical features with translation-aware spatial inductive bias.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 9 convolutional networks; AIMA neural network / convolutional network topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Shared local filters, grid/signal processing, and hierarchical feature extraction are directly supported; 'translation-aware spatial inductive bias' is synthesized from convolutional structure.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Convolutional neural networks is to connect Convolution & local filters, Pooling & local aggregation to downstream capabilities including ResNets & DenseNets, U-Nets & encoder–decoder convnets, Generative adversarial networks.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 9 convolution/pooling; deep convolutional model context including modern CNN architectures and generative models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Convolution and pooling dependencies are supported, and ResNet/GAN connections are plausible, but U-Net and the full downstream list are not directly established by the cited packet source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning convolutional network and residual network context.
sentence_reviews.notes: ResNets/DenseNets are convolutional architecture families in common vision use, though DenseNet may not be directly covered by the packet source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning convolutional network and encoder-decoder/generative model context; no direct U-Net source in packet.
sentence_reviews.notes: CNNs are a foundation for U-Net-style encoder-decoder convnets, but the packet evidence does not directly cite U-Net.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning convolutional network and generative adversarial network chapters/sections.
sentence_reviews.notes: Convolutional networks are commonly used in image GANs, but GANs do not require CNNs and the substrate wording is broad.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Convolution & local filters, Pooling & local aggregation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning convolutional networks, pooling, regularization/generalization, and optimization context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are supported; generic limitation wording is broader than direct packet evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Convolutional neural networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Convolution & local filters.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 9 convolutional networks and convolution operations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Convolution/local filters are defining CNN components.

sentence_reviews.text: Convolutional neural networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Pooling & local aggregation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Ch. 9 pooling in convolutional networks.
sentence_reviews.notes: Pooling is a common CNN component, though modern CNNs can omit explicit pooling.

sentence_reviews.text: Convolutional neural networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Normalization & standardization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning normalization and convolutional-network training context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Normalization is common in modern CNNs but not a defining or necessary component of CNNs.

sentence_reviews.text: Convolutional neural networks follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning supervised convolutional-network examples; AIMA neural-network learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: CNNs are often supervised image classifiers, but they are also used in self-supervised, generative, and reinforcement-learning contexts.

edge_reviews.notes: Convolution/local filtering is defining for CNNs; composes/core is supported.
Pooling/local aggregation is a common CNN component, though not mandatory; composes/common is supported.
Normalization is common in modern CNN training but not inherent to CNNs; relationship type is plausible, common strength is only partially supported by broad packet evidence.
Supervised CNN classifiers are common, but CNNs are model architectures rather than a training setup and are used outside supervised learning; common strength is not fully supported.
